contract.goal: Ship linear-loom project status <slug> — operator-facing summary that names the current phase, lists active Sub-Issues (Linear tasks), and emits a single-line glance summary. Phase 3 closer.
acceptance_criteria: cli/verbs/project.ts exports projectStatus; PROJECT_VERBS now [create, read, status].
Reads marker; on missing surfaces marker-unreadable.
Queries Linear in one GraphQL call: project (with projectMilestones) AND issues (filtered by the loom-project:<slug> label, ordered by updatedAt, first 50).
Derives current_phase: earliest in-progress milestone → fallback earliest not-started → null when all done.
Filters active tasks: state.type ∉ {completed, canceled}; caps at ACTIVE_TASK_LIMIT (20).
Maps active tasks to {identifier, title, state: state.name, url, updated_at: updatedAt}.
Output: {schema_version: 1, slug, title, linear_url, current_phase, active_tasks, active_task_count, summary}.
summary template: 'Phase N — <name> (<status>). <count> active task[s].' or 'No active or upcoming phase. <count> active task[s].'
rules_applied: DESIGN.md § 11 (Linear state authority — milestone state and Sub-Issue workflow state).
disqualifiers: Implementing 'recent comments' display — explicitly deferred to Phase 6 when the comment-write verbs land (no v1 comments to show yet).
inputs: PLAN.md Phase 3 D3 (operator-facing summary of active tasks, current phase, recent comments)
U3's parsePhaseFromMilestoneName + mapMilestoneStateToPhaseStatus helpers (reused for current_phase derivation)

actions: Added LinearProjectStatusResult type covering project (with milestones) + issues (filtered + ordered) in one GraphQL doc. ACTIVE_TASK_LIMIT=20 constant. PROJECT_STATUS_QUERY composes project lookup + issues lookup in one round trip.

Implemented projectStatus async verb: marker read → auth resolve → single GraphQL call → derive current_phase (in-progress > not-started > null) → filter + cap active tasks → assemble output with summary template.
summary template: 'Phase N — <name> (<status>). <count> active task[s].' for the typical case; 'No active or upcoming phase. <count> active task[s].' when no phase remains. Pluralization handled.
Added status to PROJECT_VERBS; CLI dispatch now reports [create, read, status] in candidates list.
+8 unit tests: missing-slug, marker-unreadable, happy-path-summary, fallback-to-not-started, null-phase-when-all-done, state-type filtering (active vs completed/canceled), 20-task cap, --pretty.

Recent comments deferred (PLAN.md asked for them; DESIGN.md § 7 says checkins are Sub-Issue comments; comment-write verbs are Phase 6, so no linear-loom-authored comments exist yet to surface). Documented in disqualifiers + notes.
Verified: npm test → 708 passing (was 700; +8). linear-loom project → candidates [create, read, status]. linear-loom project status → missing-slug.
Panel: all 4 evaluators approved on first run. Tighter packets continue to keep test-unit within its turn budget.

changes_since_previous: Second unit on the project-reads branch. U3 shipped project read + JSON Schema contract; this unit closes Phase 3 with the operator-facing status verb. All four Phase 3 lifecycle verbs (configure / create / read / status) now wired.
notes_for_pr: Recent-comments display deferred to Phase 6. PLAN.md Phase 3 D3 listed 'recent comments' alongside 'active tasks' and 'current phase', but DESIGN.md § 7 puts check-ins as comments on Sub-Issues — the actual comment-write verbs ship in Phase 6 (checkin write, task comment). In v1 there are no linear-loom-authored comments to surface; showing only operator-written comments would be lower-signal than waiting. The status verb's output shape leaves room for a future `recent_comments` field without a breaking change.
Single GraphQL doc covers both the project lookup (with milestones) and the labeled Sub-Issue listing — cuts round-trips from 2 to 1 and shares any rate-limit cost between the two reads.
current_phase derivation uses the same parsePhaseFromMilestoneName helper U3 added — reuse keeps the slug-prefix filtering logic in one place so both verbs see the same milestone subset.

ACTIVE_TASK_LIMIT=20 is a substrate-default that should be operator-controllable later (a --limit flag would land cleanly). Phase 3 left it as a constant; no operator has hit the cap during planning, so prematurely optimising the affordance felt like scope creep.

Sandbox smoke testing (operator-side): the issues filter shape `{ labels: { name: { eq: $labelName } } }` mirrors Linear's documented IssueFilter schema, but only a live workspace confirms the field-by-field correctness. Mocked unit tests verify request structure + response handling but not Linear's actual schema.
